feedback.text: I am Nico Saarela from Finland. I think Digital Fairness Act should move encompass the medium of video games and their preservation in addition to it's present function. Companies should not dictate the ownership of products fairly bought. Games sold as online only experiences either have to have a feasible end-of-service roadmap ensuring the customer still has access to the product they own. If this cannot be accomplished, the company should relinquish their license, leaving the service free to be provided by a willing third-party. Barring that, the purchase should be refunded monetarily. In January of 2026, a game called Anthem that I've paid 80 euros to own, will have it's servers shuttered without offline modes of play. No recompense is being offered to customers in a situation, which essentially boils down to a corporate suit coming to my house and stealing my toaster since they deemed it had served me long enough. I hope this feedback finds you well and gives you a welcome perspective to consider. Thank you for your time.
